Tests that `Math.abs` returns `NaN` when given `NaN` as input.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test case that verifies the behavior of the `Math.abs` function when it receives `NaN` (Not-a-Number) as an input. The `assert` statement checks that the result of `Math.abs(NaN)` is `NaN`, which is consistent with the expected behavior of the `Math.abs` function when handling non-numeric values. This test ensures that the function correctly returns `NaN` in such cases, maintaining the integrity of mathematical operations in JavaScript. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on this single aspect of the `Math.abs` function.
